title: Uragoga langsdorffii
taxonomy: Uragoga langsdorffii is classified in the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes over 5,000 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Uragoga langsdorffii itself is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification within the Apocynaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group. The precise classification of Uragoga langsdorffii at the genus and species levels within the Apocynaceae family may be subject to ongoing research and revision.
uses: Uragoga langsdorffii, also known as the Langsdorff's guava, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Uragoga langsdorffii is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. In some cultures, Uragoga langsdorffii is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Uragoga langsdorffii.
benefits: Uragoga langsdorffii, also known as the Yellow Passion Fruit, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. constituents: Uragoga langsdorffii contains iridoid glycosides, primarily asperuloside and isoverrucoside A, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. preparations: Uragoga langsdorffii is a plant that has been traditionally used in various herbal preparations, particularly in the Amazon region.
